requirement.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list

results.evidence: The image shows a list of items but it is not clear if they are organized hierarchically or not.
results.notes: To assess this requirement, I would need to be able to see the notes themselves and their structure. It appears that there are several bullet points in the list, but without knowing how those relate to each other or whether they can be expanded or collapsed, it's impossible to determine if they are structured hierarchically.

requirement.description: Notes are displayed with their titles visible

results.evidence: In the screenshot provided, the titles of the notes are not visible in the app on an iPhone. Only their content is displayed.
results.notes: It's possible that there might be a setting or interface element that allows users to toggle between showing note titles and their content. However, based on this image alone, the requirement 'Notes are displayed with their titles visible' cannot be met.

requirement.description: User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a navigation menu on the left side of the screen with a 'View Notes' category. However, it is not clear if this menu can be accessed while in the current app state or if there are other means to view notes such as through tapping on the notes within the main content area.
results.notes: The navigation menu does not seem to have any specific items related to 'View Notes', so it is unclear how this requirement is met. Additionally, it is possible that the app offers other ways to browse or view notes beyond what is visible in this screenshot.

requirement.description: User can access a search function

results.evidence: The image shows a mobile device screen with a keyboard and a list of tasks on it. At the bottom of the list, there is an icon with a magnifying glass next to it, which typically indicates a search function. This suggests that the user can access a search function within the app.
results.notes: While I cannot see the exact functionality of the search function or test its usability, the presence of a search icon in the app's navigation menu is a clear indication that a search function is available for users to utilize.

requirement.description: Search input field is visible or accessible

results.evidence: The screenshot appears to show a keyboard, but there is no search input field visible or accessible. The keyboard suggests that there is a device with a physical keyboard, which might be related to the search function, but it is not possible to determine from this image alone if the search input field is integrated into the keyboard or located elsewhere on the screen.
results.notes: It would be necessary to view other screens of the app or provide additional context to determine if the search input field meets this requirement.

requirement.description: User can access an AI chat or assistant feature

evidence.screenshot: A chat bubble or messaging feature is visible on a mobile phone screen, indicating an AI chat or assistant feature that allows for user interaction and communication with the AI.

results.notes: It's important to note that while the chat bubble is present in the screenshot, it's not possible to confirm if the AI is fully functional based on this single image. Additional testing would be required to assess its capabilities and effectiveness.

requirement.description: Chat interface has an input field for questions

evidence.input_field: Yes, there is an input field for questions at the bottom of the screenshot.

notes.additional_observations: The keyboard is visible in front of the input field, indicating that this is a text entry area where users can type messages or questions to the chatbot.

requirement.description: User can see a button or option to create a new note

results.evidence: The image does not show a button or option to create a new note.
results.notes: The user interface shown in the screenshot does not provide an obvious way for users to create a new note. It appears to be a list of project updates or news articles rather than a note-taking application.

requirement.description: New note creation is accessible from the main screen

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a visible 'Create Note' option on the main screen of the app, indicating that new note creation is accessible from this screen.
results.notes: It's important to note that while the 'Create Note' option is present on the main screen, it's not explicitly clear if the user can access this feature directly from this screen. Further testing would be needed to confirm the full functionality of the 'Create Note' process.

requirement.description: Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy

results.evidence: The image shows a list on an iOS device's screen. There are items within the list that have sub-items, but it is not clear from this screenshot if these sub-items can be collapsed or expanded to show nested content. The user interface elements suggest that there is some form of indentation and nesting at least with the 'Test Goals' item, which has two sub-items. However, without further interaction or additional screenshots, it is difficult to fully assess whether this app supports full-fledged hierarchy or just a simple list with sub-items. The presence of text and images within the list items suggests that the app can handle some level of nesting for content display.
results.notes: To determine if the requirement is fully met, additional screenshots or interaction with the app would be necessary to assess whether users can expand and collapse sections, navigate through a nested structure, and create new levels within the list.

requirement.description: Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes

results.notes: The screenshot shows that nested notes (MMA TMS 162.03.15) are present and indented, which aligns with the requirement for indentation of nested notes. However, there is no visible folder structure. The keyboard layout indicates that users can add or edit a note, but the 'Project' field does not provide information about project organization or nesting. To fully meet this requirement, additional UI elements such as folders and clearer visual indicators for nesting would be necessary.

requirement.description: Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a screen of a note editing application on an iPhone. There is a visible keyboard at the bottom of the screen, which suggests that there might be an editor area in this app. However, without additional context or information about the user interface, it is not possible to definitively conclude whether the editor area is 'visible' when viewing/editing a note.
results.notes: The visible keyboard indicates that editing capabilities are present but does not provide enough information about whether the editing area is actually visible when necessary. Additional testing or further investigation would be required to fully evaluate this requirement.

requirement.description: Formatting toolbar or options are available

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a formatting toolbar at the top of the screen in the block editor view with various formatting options such as bold, italic, and underline. Additionally, there are options to change the text color and background color.
results.notes: The presence of a formatting toolbar indicates that users can easily access and apply formatting styles to their content within the block editor.

requirement.description: User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)

evidence.textBlockVisibility: Paragraphs and headings are visible on the screen.

results.notes: The screenshot displays a list of tasks or actions with associated descriptions. The content appears to be structured in a block-based format, as indicated by the clear separation between headings and paragraphs. Additionally, there is a keyboard overlay at the bottom, which could suggest that editing capabilities are available, further supporting the expectation of block-level content manipulation.

requirement.description: App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile application with a hamburger menu at the bottom, which is visible and accessible on the screen.
results.notes: The app has a bottom navigation bar that allows users to switch between different sections of the app.

requirement.description: Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile

results.evidence: In the image provided, all touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile use. The buttons are of a reasonable size and spacing to accommodate typical mobile user interaction. The text is also clear and legible, indicating that the content is easily accessible.
results.notes: The app appears to be designed with user experience in mind, as indicated by the use of appropriate touch targets. Additionally, there seems to be adequate padding and margin around the elements to provide a comfortable user interface. The app's design suggests that it has been optimized for mobile screens, ensuring that users can easily interact with all elements.

requirement.description: Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

results.evidence: The content in the image is displayed within a single container without horizontal scrolling, ensuring that it's readable on the screen as required.
